evals.prompt: I need to set up an Azure Tables client in Python. My app runs in Azure with managed identity, but I also want it to work locally with Azurite. Show me how to create a TableServiceClient with DefaultAzureCredential for production and from_connection_string for local dev, then create a table called 'sessions' if it doesn't already exist.
evals.expected_output: Python code with conditional client creation using DefaultAzureCredential for production (Azure Storage endpoint) and from_connection_string for local dev. Uses create_table_if_not_exists('sessions'). Should show dual-auth pattern.

assertions.description: Uses DefaultAzureCredential() as credential parameter for the production TableServiceClient
Uses from_connection_string() for the local/Azurite path
Calls create_table_if_not_exists('sessions') rather than create_table() which raises on duplicate
Azure Storage endpoint uses correct format: https://<account>.table.core.windows.net/

evals.prompt: Write a Python function to manage user profile entities in an Azure Table called 'profiles'. I need: create a profile, get a profile by user_id (return None if not found), update just the email field without touching other fields, upsert a profile, and delete a profile. Each profile has fields: user_id (RowKey), tenant_id (PartitionKey), email, display_name, active.
evals.expected_output: Python class or functions for CRUD on profile entities. Uses create_entity, get_entity(partition_key, row_key), update_entity with UpdateMode.MERGE for partial update, upsert_entity, delete_entity. get returns None on ResourceNotFoundError. PartitionKey=tenant_id, RowKey=user_id.

assertions.description: get_profile catches ResourceNotFoundError and returns None (not raises exception)
Email-only update uses UpdateMode.MERGE (not UpdateMode.REPLACE which would delete other fields)
Entity uses PartitionKey=tenant_id and RowKey=user_id (capitalized, correct field mapping)
Upsert operation uses upsert_entity() method (not create + update conditional logic)

evals.prompt: I have a large Azure Table of orders. Write Python code to: (1) query all orders for a specific customer_id (PartitionKey) where status equals 'pending' and total_amount is greater than 100, using parameterized OData query; (2) batch-insert a list of new orders (all for the same customer_id) into the table using submit_transaction, handling TableTransactionError.
evals.expected_output: Code using query_entities with OData filter string and parameters dict (not f-string interpolation). Batch insert uses submit_transaction with list of (operation, entity) tuples. Error handling catches TableTransactionError. Operations formatted as ('create', entity_dict) tuples.

assertions.description: query_entities uses a parameters dict like parameters={'status': 'pending', 'min': 100} rather than f-string/string interpolation
submit_transaction receives a list of (operation_type, entity_dict) tuples e.g. [('create', {...}), ...]
Code catches TableTransactionError (not generic Exception or HttpResponseError) for batch failures
OData filter string uses @param placeholders like 'Status eq @status and TotalAmount gt @min' matching the parameters dict keys
